task:
Fix ticket 0032: a priority file under cocoder/priorities/*.md can exist with no entry in cocoder/priorities/order.json (a hidden/ghost priority). Add a GUARD + proving test so every created priority is visible by construction. Do NOT change runtime serving behavior (readPriorities must still append unlisted priorities at runtime) and do NOT break existing tests.

CONTEXT (verified this run): There is currently NO guard. packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts:51 `readPriorities` reads all *.md, applies order.json via `applyManifestOrder` (line 25), and silently ignores mismatches. loadPriority lives at packages/core/src/priorities/loader.ts:42. order.json currently lists: founder-stop-control, launch-disposition-first, new-primary-root, model-layer, ripgrep-dependency-research, priority-audit, oz-file-access, oz-autonomy. On disk the only loadable priority NOT in order.json is `adhoc-session` (the intentional pseudo-priority titled 'Session without a named priority' — it must NOT be flagged). AGENTS.md has no frontmatter and is already skipped by loadPriority.

IMPLEMENT:
1. A single pure function — strongest home is packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts (colocated with applyManifestOrder, which already owns order.json semantics): export `findOrphanedPriorities(prioritiesDir: string): Promise<string[]>` (or sync if simpler). It must: read every *.md in prioritiesDir; attempt to load each via the existing loadPriority (files that fail to load — e.g. AGENTS.md, README.md — are ignored, NOT orphans); read order.json if present; and return the sorted list of loadable priority ids that are NOT in order.json AND NOT placed under cocoder/priorities/archive/ or cocoder/priorities/backlog/ AND NOT in an explicit allowlist constant `INTENTIONALLY_UNLISTED_PRIORITY_IDS = ['adhoc-session']` (define it with a one-line comment explaining adhoc-session is the runtime no-named-priority pseudo-priority). This function is the SINGLE OWNER of the orphan rule — do not duplicate the rule elsewhere.
2. A unit test (packages/daemon/tests/) using a temp fixtures dir that PROVES the guard: create a priorities dir with order.json listing one id and a second *.md priority file NOT listed -> assert findOrphanedPriorities returns exactly that orphan id; then add the orphan to order.json (or move it under archive/) -> assert it returns []. Also assert a file with no valid frontmatter (AGENTS.md-style) is never reported, and that an id in the allowlist (adhoc-session) is never reported.
3. A governance test (packages/daemon/tests/) that runs findOrphanedPriorities against the REAL repo dir cocoder/priorities/ and asserts it returns [] — this is the content-pinned commit gate that fails CI if any future priority lands unlisted. Resolve the repo path from the test file location, not an absolute machine path.
4. Record the invariant: add a short decision record under cocoder/decisions/ (follow the existing ADR file naming/format in that dir) stating the 'priority visibility invariant' — every loadable priority must be registered in order.json or explicitly archived/backlogged or in the named allowlist; enforced by findOrphanedPriorities + the governance test. Reference ticket 0032 and relate to ADR-0010 (priority/order ownership). Keep it terse.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE TO REPORT:
- The exact test command(s) and their PASS output for the daemon package test suite (e.g. pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test or the repo's actual command — discover it).
- Show the unit test genuinely fails on an orphan fixture and passes once registered (the test encodes both directions).
- Governance test passes against the live cocoder/priorities/ tree (proving no current orphan; adhoc-session correctly excluded).
- typecheck passes for changed packages.
MUST NOT BREAK: packages/daemon/tests/read-surfaces.test.ts and existing readPriorities runtime behavior. Do NOT modify createPriority/route behavior in this atom. WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts, packages/daemon/tests/**, cocoder/decisions/**. Do not run git or commit.

Re-scope of the rejected atom for ticket 0032. The orphan-priority GUARD you built is correct and all 297 daemon tests pass — KEEP it exactly: `findOrphanedPriorities`, the `INTENTIONALLY_UNLISTED_PRIORITY_IDS` allowlist, the `readPriorityFiles` extraction, packages/daemon/tests/priority-order.test.ts, cocoder/decisions/0038-priority-visibility-invariant.md, and the ADR-0010/README edits. The rejection is purely about TWO out-of-scope extras that must be REVERTED so only the guard lands:

1. REVERT the writeOrder semantic change. Restore the ORIGINAL `writeOrder` behavior that FILTERS the requested order down to valid ids only (the pre-atom version: `const order = requestedOrder.filter((id) => validIds.has(id))` with a `ReadonlySet<string>` validIds param). DELETE the new `completeOrder` helper entirely. `writePriorityOrder` and `writeTicketOrder` must behave EXACTLY as before this atom — they must not append unlisted ids. (Reason: changing the reorder endpoints' behavior — including ticket reordering — is unrelated to ticket 0032 and is untested.) If reverting writeOrder requires `writePriorityOrder`/`writeTicketOrder` to pass a Set again, restore those call sites to their original form too. You MAY keep the internal use of `readPriorityFiles` (it is behavior-preserving) — just ensure the validIds set/filtering semantics match the original.

2. DELETE the unused `export async function registerPriorityInOrder(...)` entirely — it has zero callers and is out of scope for this atom.

NET RESULT: the only behavior change versus the pre-atom tree is the addition of the read-only `findOrphanedPriorities` guard + its tests + the decision-record docs. No change to readPriorities, writePriorities/writeOrder/writePriorityOrder/writeTicketOrder runtime behavior.

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: run the daemon test suite and paste the PASS summary (must be all green, including tests/priority-order.test.ts and tests/read-surfaces.test.ts); run typecheck for the daemon package and report it green; and show a `git diff` of packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts confirming writeOrder is back to filter-only semantics, completeOrder is gone, and registerPriorityInOrder is gone. WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts, packages/daemon/tests/**, cocoder/decisions/**. Do not run git commit.

Close ticket 0032 (the priority-visibility / ghost-priority ticket) through the repo's established ticket-close path, now that the fix is verified and committed (commit 4819767, ADR-0038, the findOrphanedPriorities guard + daemon governance test).

FIRST discover the exact close convention — do NOT invent one. Find the ticket file under cocoder/tickets/ (e.g. cocoder/tickets/0032-*.md) and inspect at least one ALREADY-CLOSED ticket in cocoder/tickets/ to learn the precise convention: the frontmatter `state` value used for closed tickets, any resolution/closed-on fields, the resolution-note format, and whether closing also removes the id from cocoder/tickets/order.json (check readTickets / writeTicketOrder semantics — open tickets are those with state === 'open'). Match that convention exactly.

THEN close ticket 0032 following it:
1. Set its frontmatter state to the closed value the convention uses (matching existing closed tickets byte-for-byte in field shape).
2. Add a terse resolution note in the body (matching how other closed tickets record resolution) stating: resolved by the orphan-priority visibility guard — `findOrphanedPriorities` in packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts plus the daemon governance test that fails when any loadable cocoder/priorities/*.md is unlisted/unarchived/un-allowlisted; recorded in ADR-0038; the two named orphans oz-file-access and oz-autonomy are already registered in cocoder/priorities/order.json. Cite commit 4819767.
3. If the convention removes closed tickets from cocoder/tickets/order.json, remove 0032 from it; otherwise leave order.json as the convention dictates.

Do NOT reopen the code fix or touch packages/**. 

EVIDENCE TO REPORT: the git diff of the ticket file (and tickets/order.json if changed); confirmation that the closed-state field matches the existing-closed-ticket convention; and run the affected ticket/governance test suite (discover and run it — e.g. the daemon read-surfaces/tickets tests or any ticket loader test) and paste the PASS summary, proving readTickets no longer surfaces 0032 as open and nothing is left red. WRITE SCOPE: cocoder/tickets/**. Do not run git commit.
